stories.title: WCWS Road Opens — Day 1 of 2026 NCAA Regionals
stories.angle: The 2026 NCAA Tournament field of 64 opens today at 16 regional sites. Top seeds Alabama (No. 1), Texas (No. 2), Oklahoma (No. 3), Nebraska (No. 4) all open play. Key games include Alabama vs USC Upstate at Noon CT (SEC Network), Oklahoma vs Binghamton at 2:30 PM CT, Texas vs Wagner at 3 PM CT (ESPN+), Nebraska vs South Dakota at 5:30 PM ET. Super Regionals May 21–24; WCWS May 28–June 5 in OKC.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
It's tournament day. 🥎

64 teams. 16 regionals. One dream — Oklahoma City.

Alabama, Texas, Oklahoma, Nebraska all take the field TODAY.

The road to the WCWS starts right now. 🏆

#NCAAsoftball #WCWS2026 #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Every year someone says a top seed is "safe."

Every year a regional shocks everyone.

Today starts the chaos. 👀

Who gets upset in the first round? Let us know. 👇

#NCAAsoft #WCWS #Softball

stories.title: South Dakota Makes History — Cinderella Faces Nebraska in Lincoln
stories.angle: South Dakota (20-34-1) is making their first-ever NCAA Tournament appearance in program history (DI or DII) since the program launched in 1978. They won four games in four days at the Summit League Tournament, including two consecutive wins over top seed Omaha. Madison Evans was the All-Tournament MVP. Seven Nebraska natives on the roster return home for the Lincoln Regional. They open vs No. 4 seed Nebraska tonight (6:30 PM ET).

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
South Dakota's first-ever NCAA Tournament game is TONIGHT. 🐾

Founded 1978. Never made the NCAAs until now.

They won the Summit League Tournament to get here.

Tonight: No. 4 Nebraska in Lincoln. 5:30 PM ET.

#SouthDakota #LincolnRegional #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Oklahoma Opens Norman Regional — Gasso Hunts 9th Championship
stories.angle: Oklahoma (48-8, No. 3 national seed) opens at Love's Field today (2:30 PM CT) vs Binghamton. Patty Gasso has 8 national titles; pursuing 9th. Sooners lead nation in BA (.389) and have 174 HRs. 9 consecutive CWS appearances since 2015. Lost in 2025 WCWS to Texas — today is about redemption.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma lost the 2025 WCWS to Texas.

Patty Gasso's Sooners open at Love's Field today — and they want that title BACK.

8 national championships. .389 team BA — best in the nation.

Redemption starts now. 🌪️

#OUSoftball #NormanRegional #WCWS2026
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW UP (new game-day angle from May 13 Dynasty coverage)

stories.title: FSU's 12th Straight Regional — Noles Open as No. 9 Seed in Tallahassee
stories.angle: Florida State (49-8, No. 9 national seed) hosts their 12th consecutive regional in Tallahassee, opening vs Stetson (36-20) at Noon ET Friday. FSU won their 20th ACC championship this year (2-1 over Virginia Tech), went 21-3 in ACC play, and are one of the deepest programs in America. Hosting 12th straight despite being only the No. 9 seed — rare.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Florida State: 49-8.

20th ACC Tournament championship.

12th consecutive NCAA Regional host.

And they're only the No. 9 national seed?

The Noles open vs Stetson today at noon ET and they've got something to prove. 🍢

#FSUSoftball #TallahasseeRegional #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: Texas Defends — Longhorns Open Austin Regional vs Wagner
stories.angle: Texas (No. 2 seed, 26th NCAA Tournament appearance) defends their 2025 national title opening vs NEC champion Wagner at McCombs Field, 3 PM CT, on ESPN+. Pitcher Citlaly Kavan (who struck out 12 in the SEC title game) leads a staff that won the 2025 national championship and the first-ever SEC title. Texas enters having won the 2025 WCWS and the 2026 SEC championship — entering today as the hunted, not the hunter.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Texas walks into 2026 as defending national champions.

They're the No. 2 seed. They won the SEC title.

Citlaly Kavan struck out 12 in the championship game. 🤘

Title defense begins today in Austin.

#TexasSoftball #AustinRegional #WCWS2026

stories.title: Weekend Watch Guide — Best Regional Matchups May 15–17

Regional, 7 PM PT, ESPN2), Arkansas vs Fordham (Fayetteville, 4:30 PM ET, ESPN+), FSU vs Stetson (Tallahassee, 12 PM ET, ESPN+). Upset watch: VT at LSU's regional, Mississippi State vs Oregon in Austin, Indiana at Tennessee Regional.
